title: Immunostaining of DDR proteins in senescent cells

steps: Plate 15-20 x 10 3 cells on coverslips 1 day before the staining
Fix and permeabilize cells for 2’ with methanol/aceton 1:1 at RT
Stain with primary antibodies diluted in PBG1x at indicated concentration for 1h at RT in a humidified chamber
Stain with secondary antibodies diluted in PBG1x for 1h at RT in a dark humidified chamber
Wash cells gently 2x5’ in PBG1x and 2x5’ in PBS1x
Stain with Dapi for 5’ in the dark
Mount coverslips with 3_ul of Mowiol mounting medium
Let the coverslips dry before microscope analysis
Using different type of fixative can allow better staining depending on antibody Lipofuscin accumulation in senescent cells can give an high citosolic autofluorescent background material
Use always a positive (X-Rays treated cells 2-5 Gy) and a negative control (proliferating or even better contact inhibited (for a short period) quiescient cells) to reduce background
Oncogene-induced senescence is a DNA damage response triggered by DNA hyper-replication by Di Micco, R. et al. published in Nature on 03 Oct, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature05327
Figure 1. Confocal analysis of DDR proteins (&#x3B3;H2AX=green and 53BP1=red) in oncogene-induced senescent cells. In blue dapi staining.
